title: Horse RPG
description: Magical summoning of a loyal steed: use the scroll to call your horse to any point in the world.
body:
![banner](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/589cbd2172d6b9f496c63790661b7d69264016e5.png)
### 🐎 HorseRPG: Your Loyal Steed, Always by Your Side!
**HorseRPG** elevates traditional horse riding into a true RPG-style mechanic. Your mount is no longer just a mob—it becomes a magical companion bound to you. Never worry about losing your horse in the woods or leaving it at a cave entrance again.

### ✨ Key Features
- **📜 Summoning Scroll (Horse Scroll)**: Use a magical scroll to instantly summon your horse to your location anywhere in the world.

- **💾 Full NBT Persistence**: The mod saves every detail of your horse using the NBT system. Health, speed, jump height, and inventory contents are all kept safe.
- **🛡️ Auto-Save Protection**: If you move too far away (over 20 blocks), your horse will vanish in a puff of smoke, "retracting" into the data storage. This protects it from accidental death and reduces server lag.
- **🌟 Magical Visuals**: Summoning and despawning are accompanied by custom particles and sound effects. Summoned horses are highlighted with a Glow effect, making them easy to spot in the dark.

### 🧩 Compatibility
HorseRPG is built with flexibility in mind. Because it handles the complete NBT data dump of the entity:

- **UI Compatibility**: The mod is designed to be compatible with any other mods that modify the horse interface (adding new slots, visual elements, or extra inventory tabs).
- **Modded Attributes**: If other mods add custom armor or unique stats to horses, HorseRPG will preserve them during "despawn" and restore them upon the next summon.

### 🛠 Admin Commands
To keep the world tidy:


```
/horserpg clearworld
```
 — Instantly removes all active RPG horses from the current world (they remain safely in player storage; they are only cleared from the physical world).

### 📥 Requirements
- Minecraft: 
```
1.21.1
```

- Fabric Loader:
```
 >=0.18.5
```

- Java: 
```
21
```
